userStories.title: Add bedrock provider type
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'bedrock' to the Provider union type in src/types.ts (between 'anthropic' and 'openai')
Do NOT add a new ApiFormat — Bedrock reuses 'anthropic-messages'
pnpm build compiles without errors
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: Minimal change — types only. Bedrock uses the same request/response body format as the Anthropic Messages API, so no new ApiFormat is needed.

userStories.title: Add Bedrock detection in routing
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'model' to API_PATH_SEGMENTS in src/core/routing.ts (~line 15-27) so /model/anthropic.claude.../invoke isn't treated as a source tag
Add Bedrock detection in classifyRequest() — insert AFTER Anthropic detection and BEFORE OpenAI detection (~line 90)
Detection signals: (a) path matches /model/{id}/invoke or /model/{id}/converse, (b) authorization header starts with 'AWS4-HMAC-SHA256'
classifyRequest returns { provider: 'bedrock', apiFormat: 'anthropic-messages' } for Bedrock requests
Add Bedrock case in resolveTargetUrl() — route to upstreams.bedrock or default 'https://bedrock-runtime.us-east-1.amazonaws.com'
Existing provider detection (anthropic, openai, gemini, vertex, chatgpt) is NOT affected
Add routing tests in test/routing.test.ts: detect from /model/{id}/invoke path, /model/{id}/converse path, SigV4 auth header, apiFormat returns 'anthropic-messages', resolveTargetUrl routes correctly
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: Detection regex: /\/model\/[^/]+\/(invoke|converse)/. Place after Anthropic detection so /v1/messages still routes to anthropic. For resolveTargetUrl, add `else if (provider === 'bedrock')` block following the pattern of existing providers.

userStories.title: Handle Bedrock in request parsing and model ID normalization
acceptanceCriteria: In src/core/parse.ts, change the Anthropic provider check (~line 207) to: if (provider === 'anthropic' || provider === 'bedrock')
Add Bedrock model ID normalization after parsing: strip region prefix (us., eu.), vendor prefix (anthropic.), and version suffix (-v1:0, -v2:0)

Normalization examples: 'anthropic.claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022-v2:0' → 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', 'us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-20250514-v1:0' → 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514', 'anthropic.claude-3-haiku-20240307-v1:0' → 'claude-3-haiku-20240307'
Standard (non-Bedrock) model IDs pass through unchanged
In src/analysis/ingest.ts, extract model from Bedrock URL path /model/{modelId}/invoke when body.model is missing

Add parse tests in test/context-parse.test.ts: Bedrock request body parses correctly (reuse anthropic fixture with provider='bedrock'), model ID normalization for all three formats above, standard IDs unchanged
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: The body structure (system, tools, messages) is identical to Anthropic — only the provider check and model normalization differ. Normalization regex chain: .replace(/^[a-z]{2}\./, '').replace(/^anthropic\./, '').replace(/-v\d+:\d+$/, ''). For ingest.ts, use: const modelMatch = capture.path.match(/\/model\/([^/]+)\//) to extract from URL.

userStories.title: Update source detection for Bedrock
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'bedrock' to the PROVIDER_NAMES set in src/core/source.ts (~line 40-45)
'bedrock' is not used as a source tag (it's a provider, not a tool)
Add test in test/source.test.ts (or relevant test file) confirming 'bedrock' is excluded from source tags
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: Small change. PROVIDER_NAMES is a Set that prevents provider names from being mistakenly used as tool source identifiers.

userStories.title: Add Bedrock capture pattern to MITM addon
acceptanceCriteria: In mitm_addon.py, add Bedrock CAPTURE_PATTERN: ('bedrock-runtime.', '/model/', 'bedrock', None) — insert before catch-all patterns (~line 38-52)
Add Bedrock to _detect_api_format(): if '/model/' in path and ('/invoke' in path or '/converse' in path) return 'anthropic-messages'

Add _parse_aws_eventstream(raw_bytes) function that parses AWS EventStream binary format into SSE text lines — handles 4-byte total_len, 4-byte headers_len, prelude CRC, payload extraction, base64 'bytes' field decoding
In the response() function, detect 'application/vnd.amazon.eventstream' content type and parse with _parse_aws_eventstream, set response_is_streaming=True
Add safe Bedrock response headers to the allowlist: x-amzn-requestid, x-amzn-bedrock-invocation-latency, x-amzn-bedrock-input-token-count, x-amzn-bedrock-output-token-count
pnpm test passes (existing tests not broken)
userStories.notes: Bedrock streaming uses binary AWS EventStream format (vnd.amazon.eventstream), NOT SSE like other providers. EventStream frames: [4B total_len][4B headers_len][4B prelude_crc][headers][payload][4B message_crc]. Payload is JSON with a 'bytes' field containing base64-encoded SSE event data. Use struct.unpack('>I', ...) for big-endian uint32. Source is None because detectSource() will identify the tool (e.g. 'claude') from headers/system prompts.

userStories.title: Add Bedrock response model normalization
acceptanceCriteria: In src/lhar/response.ts, in parseResponseUsage(), normalize Bedrock model IDs in the response model field using the same logic as US-003
Extract the normalization into a shared helper function normalizeBedrockModelId() — place in src/core/models.ts or a small utility module
Both parse.ts and response.ts use the shared helper (refactor parse.ts to use it too)
Add tests for the shared helper covering all model ID formats plus pass-through for standard IDs
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: DRY up the normalization from US-003. The helper should be: if the ID contains 'anthropic.' then strip region prefix, vendor prefix, version suffix. Otherwise return unchanged. This ensures standard Anthropic model IDs (claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022) pass through.

userStories.title: Add CLI tool configuration for Bedrock
acceptanceCriteria: When CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1 or ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL is set, the 'claude' tool config switches to MITM mode (needsMitm: true, sets https_proxy and SSL_CERT_FILE in childEnv)
Add a --bedrock CLI flag in parseCliArgs() that explicitly forces MITM mode for Claude
Update help text with Bedrock examples: 'context-lens claude' (auto-detects from env vars), 'context-lens --bedrock claude' (explicit)
Add/update tests in test/cli-utils.test.ts for Bedrock env var detection and --bedrock flag parsing
pnpm test passes
userStories.notes: The auto-detection logic goes in src/cli.ts (or wherever tool config is resolved after initial lookup from TOOL_CONFIG). Check process.env.CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK === '1' || process.env.ANTHROPIC_BEDROCK_BASE_URL. When triggered, override the claude config: { childEnv: { https_proxy: MITM_PROXY_URL, SSL_CERT_FILE: '' }, extraArgs: [], serverEnv: {}, needsMitm: true }. The --bedrock flag goes in parseCliArgs() and sets a useBedrock field.

userStories.title: Final verification and pricing check
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm build compiles without errors
pnpm lint passes Biome checks
pnpm test — all existing + new tests pass
Run pnpm run check:pricing — verify normalized Bedrock model IDs map to existing Anthropic pricing entries
If pricing gaps found, document them in a comment in the code but do NOT modify @contextio/core
userStories.notes: This is the final validation story. After normalization, Bedrock model IDs should map to standard Anthropic names (e.g. claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022) which should already exist in MODEL_PRICING. If check:pricing fails or the command doesn't exist, just verify pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test all pass.
